task_completion.description: Create the survey with the correct title, group, and mandatory 0-10 NPS question.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey titled 'Q4 2024 Customer Experience NPS Survey'. In the survey structure, there should be a group named 'NPS Rating' containing a question with code 'NPS' that is marked as mandatory and uses a 0-10 scale.

task_completion.description: Create an equation question named 'NPSCat' with correct classification logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a question with code 'NPSCat' of type 'Equation'. The logic or equation field should show a formula classifying scores (e.g., Detractor for 0-6, Passive for 7-8, Promoter for 9-10).

task_completion.description: Create a 'Follow-Up Feedback' group with three Long Free Text questions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a group named 'Follow-Up Feedback' containing three questions of type Long Free Text with codes 'DetractorQ', 'PassiveQ', and 'PromoterQ'. The question texts should match the specific prompts requested for each category.

task_completion.description: Apply conditional logic to the follow-up questions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for relevance equations or condition settings on 'DetractorQ', 'PassiveQ', and 'PromoterQ' ensuring they only display for their respective NPS categories (e.g., DetractorQ shows if NPS <= 6 or NPSCat == 'Detractor').

task_completion.description: Configure survey settings to anonymize responses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey's Participant settings or General settings showing 'Anonymized responses' set to 'Yes' or 'On'.
Look for a status indicator showing the survey is 'Active', 'Running', or a button to 'Stop this survey', indicating it has been successfully activated.

integrity.description: The agent used the LimeSurvey web interface to create the survey.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LimeSurvey admin interface being navigated and forms being filled out, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the survey creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) should be visible modifying the DOM to fake the presence of the survey or questions.

integrity.description: The survey and questions were actually saved in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show success messages after saving, or the questions appearing in the survey structure overview, not just typed into unsaved forms.

privileged_info_for_vlm: LimeSurvey 6 supports Equation questions (type '*') for classification logic. The standard NPS classification ranges are Detractor: 0-6, Passive: 7-8, Promoter: 9-10. Follow-up questions should be Long Free Text (type 'T'). Conditional logic is implemented using relevance equations. Anonymized responses is a standard setting in LimeSurvey.
